steps.expected: Slice fields produce NATS array syntax [...] with correct elements and ordering
steps.observed: PASS - []string and []int slices marshal to arrays, round-trip via Parse yields correct []any with 2 routes and 3 ports

steps.expected: Non-nil empty slice without omitempty still appears in output
steps.observed: PASS - Empty []string{} produces output containing 'items' key

steps.expected: Map fields produce NATS map block syntax { key: value }
steps.observed: PASS - map[string]string marshals to block, round-trip yields equivalent map with region=us-east

steps.expected: Empty map produces valid block syntax
steps.observed: PASS - Empty map[string]string{} marshals and round-trips to empty map

steps.expected: map[string]any with mixed types marshals correctly
steps.observed: PASS - map with string, int64, bool values round-trips correctly

steps.expected: map[string]any with nested maps marshals correctly
steps.observed: PASS - map with str, num, flag, and nested map values all round-trip correctly

steps.expected: Nested structs produce correctly nested map blocks
steps.observed: PASS - Config with ClusterOpts{Name, Port} marshals to nested block, round-trip yields cluster.name=my-cluster, cluster.port=6222

steps.expected: 3+ level nesting works correctly
steps.observed: PASS - Outer>Middle>Inner struct marshals to 3-level nested blocks, round-trip yields middle.inner.val=deep

steps.expected: []map[string]string marshals as array of inline maps
steps.observed: PASS - Array of 2 maps with name/role keys marshals and round-trips as []any with 2 items

steps.expected: []Struct marshals as array of map blocks
steps.observed: PASS - []User with name/age fields marshals to array of maps, round-trip yields users[0].name=alice

assertions.title: Struct tag field naming in Marshal

steps.expected: Fields tagged conf:"custom_name" marshal using the tag name as key
steps.observed: PASS - Host tagged conf:"listen_addr" outputs listen_addr key; round-trip finds listen_addr=0.0.0.0

steps.expected: Fields tagged conf:"-" are omitted from output
steps.observed: PASS - Secret field tagged conf:"-" not found in output; password123 value absent

steps.expected: Zero-value fields with omitempty are omitted; non-zero fields present
steps.observed: PASS - name/port/debug/rate/routes (all zero) omitted; present=yes-here included in output

steps.expected: Nil pointer and nil map with omitempty are omitted
steps.observed: PASS - nil *string (ptr) and nil map[string]string (meta) both omitted from output

steps.expected: Empty string WITHOUT omitempty is NOT omitted
steps.observed: PASS - Empty Name string without omitempty tag produces 'name' key in output

steps.expected: Types implementing MarshalConfig() use custom output
steps.observed: PASS - customMarshalType with Data='custom-output' produces 'custom-output' in marshaled config

steps.expected: Pointer receiver MarshalConfig() also works
steps.observed: PASS - *customMarshalPtrType with Data='hello' produces 'ptr-custom:hello' in output

steps.expected: MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ") indents nested blocks by 2 spaces
steps.observed: PASS - Nested val indented with 2 spaces; output re-parses correctly with inner.val=test

steps.expected: MarshalIndent(v, "", "\t") uses tab indentation
steps.observed: PASS - Nested val indented with tab character; output re-parses successfully

steps.expected: 3-level nesting has correct cumulative indentation
steps.observed: PASS - Deep val has 4 spaces (2 levels × 2 spaces); round-trip yields middle.deep.val=nested

steps.expected: MarshalIndent with prefix prepends prefix to each line
steps.observed: PASS - Every non-empty line starts with '// ' prefix

summary: Tested 8 assertions (VAL-MARSHAL-007 through VAL-MARSHAL-014): all 8 passed. Ran 21 individual test functions covering slice/array marshaling, map/block marshaling, nested structs, array of maps, struct tag naming, omitempty behavior, custom Marshaler interface, and MarshalIndent indentation control. All tests produce PASS with correct round-trip behavior verified through Parse/Unmarshal.
